article_title: Increase in liquor transfers between licences
body: This change appliesonlyto the following licences: Licensees who hold multiple licences can now purchase and sell (transfer) up to $100,000 of liquor between their own licences annually (January 1 to December 31). The licences: This is an increase from the previous limit of $10,000. The change allows licensees additional flexibility to transfer their inventory between the following licence classes: This change also allows liquor retailers to transfer their inventory between their retail licences in the same class: For licensees who wish to purchase from another legal entity, the annual limit remains at $10,000. Manufacturer licensees continue to be ineligible to transfer product between licenses as this is generally incompatible with the intent of the lounge endorsement. The language in the terms and conditions is updated to reflect that the transfer of liquor between licences is legally the purchase and sale of liquor between two parties. The changes can be found in the: Both parties must keep records within their liquor registers indicating the: The liquor register must be available for inspection at all times. You can find more information about the liquor register in theterms and conditions handbookfor your licence (see the "Liquor Register" section, under "Your Liquor Licence"). Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws.

minutes_briefs.title: Liquor transfer limit between own licences raised to $100,000
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has increased the annual limit for transferring liquor between licences you own from $10,000 to $100,000. This applies to certain licence classes and gives you more flexibility to move inventory between your own locations. Transfers to or from a different legal entity (another business) remain capped at $10,000 per year.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Update your liquor register records for any transfers and ensure both parties keep detailed records of each transaction.
